Dina Titus (D-NV), says President Trump's deal with Tehran shows the administration "could have done all of this through diplomacy." stories.title: Ret. Gen. Clark: US Must Not Give Up Iran Leverage Too Soon
stories.summary: Retired General Wesley Clark, former NATO Supreme Allied Commander Europe and former Commander for US Southern Command, says the US should keep aircraft carrier groups in the Middle East through the 60-day negotiation period with Iran, warning that Washington's leverage will fall once forces depart. Clark also discusses the role of US military assistance in Ukraine's war against Russia. He speaks

stories.title: Ambassador Stefanishyna: Iran Deal Clears Path for Ukraine Focus at G7
stories.summary: Ukrainian Ambassador to the US Olha Stefanishyna says the US deal with Iran allows leaders to "fully concentrate" on Russia's war in Ukraine at this week's G7 summit, where Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is expected to meet with President Trump. Stefanishyna outlines the importance of US support, calling air defense and missiles for Patriot systems "absolutely vital" for Ukraine, and expr

stories.title: Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in its first debt sale since start of AI boom
stories.summary: Nvidia is set to raise capital in a debt sale for the first time since 2021, when the chipmaker was a fraction of its current size.

stories.title: Asian Stock Rally Cools Ahead of Rate Decisions: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian stocks wavered as investors paused to assess the durability of the relief rally fueled by the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while awaiting rate decisions in Australia and Japan.

stories.title: Oil Steadies as Traders Seek Clarity on Planned Hormuz Reopening
stories.summary: Oil steadied after the biggest drop in more than two weeks as traders, shippers and producers waited for details of a US-Iran deal that’s intended to pave the way for the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: UK to Announce Fresh Russia Sanctions, Ukraine Energy Support
stories.summary: The UK said it would impose fresh sanctions on Russia and extend further energy support to Ukraine, as Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his European allies seek to increase pressure on Vladimir Putin and encourage US President Donald Trump to push for new peace talks to end the conflict.

stories.title: Trump administration considers $300bn fund for Iran if deal is upheld
stories.summary: Incentives would be tied to Tehran’s ‘performance’ including over opening of the Strait of Hormuz and nuclear talks

stories.title: Goldman-Backed Go Soars 21% After Biggest Japan IPO This Year
stories.summary: Taxi-hailing app provider Go Inc.’s shares surged 21% in its trading debut on the Tokyo Stock Exchange on Tuesday, helping to support investor sentiment after the nation’s largest initial public offering so far this year.

stories.title: PIMCO’s Cantrill: Geopolitical Risk Not off the Table
stories.summary: Libby Cantrill, PIMCO Head of Public Policy, discussed the recent developments in negotiations involving Iran, emphasizing that what has been achieved is more of a detente than a comprehensive deal. She highlighted the uncertainty surrounding key issues such as Iran’s nuclear program, funding of proxies, and the stance of regional players like Israel. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greife

stories.title: How the Strait of Hormuz reopening could unfold if the U.S.-Iran deal is implemented
stories.summary: Tanker traffic through Hormuz could quickly increase but it is unclear whether transits will approach prewar levels, according to Kpler.

stories.title: Trump trusts Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. It matters for more than interest rates
stories.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is expected to hold interest rates steady this week, but President Donald Trump’s trust gives him room to pursue longer-term changes.

stories.title: Ebola Cases Top 800 in Congo as Aid Groups Warn Outbreak May Be Larger Than Reported
stories.summary: Ebola treatment centers in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo are becoming overwhelmed as confirmed cases surge past 800 and health workers struggle to track transmission chains, aid groups warned ahead of an emergency summit of African leaders.

stories.title: US at Odds With Allies Over How Easy Reopening Hormuz Is
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will reopen by Friday, but his European allies don't share his optimism. Bloomberg's Laura Davison has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Rep. Titus on Iran Deal: US Has 'Not Come Out Ahead'
stories.summary: Rep. Dina Titus, a Nevada Democrat and member of the House Foreign Affairs and Transportation Committees, says President Trump's deal with Tehran shows the administration "could have done all of this through diplomacy," arguing the main issues behind the Iran conflict remain unresolved and the US is "worse off than before." She also says she would "not necessarily" support an extension of Section

stories.title: Bolivian Blockades Start to Recede After 46 Days of Unrest
stories.summary: Road blockades that led to food and medicine shortages across Bolivia have begun to recede after 46 days in a conflict that tested the new president’s staying power.

stories.title: How the US and Iran Might Spin Their Deal to End the War
stories.summary: On the Big Take podcast, Sarah Holder and Bloomberg’s National Security Editor, Nick Wadhams, discuss the US-Iran ceasefire deal and how Israel’s fight with Iran’s proxies fits into the equation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Where the US, Israel and Iran Stand on a Deal to End Months of War
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take podcast, we examine the major hurdles facing a proposed US-Iran peace deal, including how Israel fits into the equation.

stories.title: The Iran deal came just in time as Strategic Petroleum Reserve hits lowest level since 1983
stories.summary: Oil executives have warned that global inventories are rapidly depleting due to the supply disruption triggered by the Iran war.

stories.title: Romania Prime Minister Designate Defies Party to Seek Government
stories.summary: Romania’s prime minister-designate, Adrian Vestea, said he will seek support from other pro-European forces for his government in an attempt to break weeks of political deadlock despite opposition from the majority of his own Liberal Party.

stories.title: JD Vance Becomes Face of Trump’s Iran Deal — and Its Risks
stories.summary: Owning the end to a war could boost the vice-president’s 2028 chances, or crush them.

stories.title: Worst of US Inflation May Be Over If Iran Peace Deal Sticks
stories.summary: The peace agreement between the US and Iran, if it holds, suggests the worst of the war-driven inflation has likely passed, but the outlook for American consumers and the broader economy remains far from certain.

stories.title: 10-year Treasury yield slides as Iran deal drives rethink on Fed interest rate hikes
stories.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield fell on Monday following the announcement of a preliminary peace agreement between Washington and Tehran.

stories.title: US Government Orders Anthropic to Disable Foreign Access to Mythos
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Maggie Eastland breaks down what's at stake as Anthropic shut down its Mythos Fable 5 model after the US government issued a directive blocking foreign nationals from accessing the artificial intelligence tool. Eastland said that what was pitched as a voluntary AI security program by Trump administration, now seems to leave Anthropic with no choice but to comply with government directi

stories.title: Rieder Says No Forward Guidance Is a Good Thing
stories.summary: Rick Rieder, BlackRock global fixed income CIO, says if the new Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh eliminates forward guidance, it will be good for markets. He speaks about how other changes Warsh makes could impact stocks, the state of private credit and where he sees the price of bitcoin heading. He appears on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Anthropic Disables AI Access for Foreign Nationals | Bloomberg Tech 6/15/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow breaks down why Anthropic disabled access to its most advanced models for all foreign nationals after a request from the Trump administration. Plus, Nvidia is seeking to raise at least $20 billion from its first corporate bond sale since 2021. And, SpaceX shares throttle up on day 2 of trading, adding to a blockbuster public markets debut on Friday. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Cohere Seeing Inbounds After Washington's Anthropic Scrutiny
stories.summary: Cohere Chief AI Officer Joelle Pineau says Cohere has seen a huge number of inbounds after Anthropic was ordered by the US government to disable access to its most advanced AI platforms for all foreign nationals, with governments and investors alike now seeking non-US models as Washington steps up its AI oversight. She joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: What Is Anthropic’s Mythos AI and Why Was It Blocked?
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC has said its new artificial intelligence tool, Mythos, is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can’t be released to the general public. The AI giant released Mythos to only 200 partner organizations because if a tool this powerful fell into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could help attackers more easily steal data or disrupt critical infras

stories.title: The Race to Build Data Centers in Space
stories.summary: “Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Anthropic to meet White House over AI tool suspension
stories.summary: The sudden meeting was called after Anthropic had to block users from just-released AI models.

stories.title: Trump at G7 Hails Iran Deal as Divisions With Tehran Remain

stories.title: Anthropic to meet with Trump administration over Mythos dispute

stories.title: US, Iran to Sign Peace Deal in Switzerland on Friday
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will be open by Friday. Officials say the US and Iran are due to sign their interim peace deal in Switzerland on Friday. For now, Trump is still at the G7 in France. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Cutting access to Anthropic’s Mythos is a gift to China
stories.summary: Washington’s suspension just strengthened Beijing’s pitch for its own competing AI models

stories.title: Nuveen Private Bond Chief Scoops Up AI’s ‘Picks and Shovels’
stories.summary: Data centers and computing chips are all the rage in global credit markets right now, but Nuveen LLC’s Laura Parrott is more excited about the “boring, plain vanilla” infrastructure on the outskirts of AI that will benefit regardless of how the technology pans out.

stories.title: Oil prices fall and shares jump after US-Iran deal announced
stories.summary: Under the agreement, the key Strait of Hormuz waterway will be reopened, US President Donald Trump said.

stories.title: Biocon's Mazumdar Shaw Says Firm On Track To Be Top Insulin Maker
stories.summary: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar Shaw says she is confident that the company's investment and capacity expansion in Insulin will make the company the top hub for the drug in the next five years. She speaks in an exclusive conversation with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: CNBC Daily Open: Relief rally rolls on as Mideast deal boosts sentiment
stories.summary: As investors look to the formal signing of the Iran deal on Friday, equities are hitting fresh highs, oil is down and yields are pulling back.

stories.title: Jardine to Buy Back $500 Million Shares, Seeks to Boost Returns
stories.summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings Ltd. will buy back $500 million of its own shares by the end of next year as the Hong Kong company lays out targets for its transformation from a long-term owner-operator into an active investor more akin to a private equity fund.

stories.title: AllianzGI Trims Bullish Yuan Bet, Turns Neutral After Rally
stories.summary: Allianz Global Investors has reduced some of its bullish yuan position and turned neutral, taking profits after a rally that made the Chinese currency Asia’s best performer this year.

stories.title: Woodside Energy’s Louisiana LNG President Abruptly Departs
stories.summary: The top executive at Woodside Energy Group Ltd.’s $17.5 billion liquefied natural gas project in Louisiana has departed, effective Monday, just over a year after she was hired, according to a company memo seen by Bloomberg.

stories.title: What is Helium-3 and could we get it from the moon?
stories.summary: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.

stories.title: Worst Indonesian Credit Volatility in Years Imperils Debt Boom
stories.summary: The highest volatility in rupiah corporate bonds in four years is threatening to dent record issuance by companies in Southeast Asia’s largest economy at a time when government policy swings have hurt international investor confidence in Indonesian assets.

stories.title: Key Republican Expresses Optimism on Trump Attorney General Pick
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s nomination of Todd Blanche to lead the US Justice Department was met with cautious optimism by a key Republican senator who has repeatedly split with the administration on other issues.

stories.title: Trader Loses $1 Million on Cabo Verde’s Stunning World Cup Tie
stories.summary: A single trader on Polymarket lost nearly $1 million when Cabo Verde fought Spain to a stunning draw on Monday in one of the most unlikely outcomes in recent World Cup history, powered by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who left the pitch in tears.

stories.title: Gold Holds Gain as Trump Touts Reopening of Hormuz This Week
stories.summary: Gold held a gain after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen on Friday, likely easing an energy and inflation shock that has roiled global markets.

stories.title: IG4 Offers to Buy Raizen Debt in Quest for Majority Stake
